exercises.name: Jump Rope / Jog in Place
form_tips: ✅ Keep a light bounce — land on balls of feet
✅ Keep arms relaxed at sides
instructions: Keep an easy pace — goal is heart rate elevation, not exhaustion.
Stay light on your feet, land softly.

exercises.name: Arm Circles
form_tips: ✅ Make large, controlled circles
✅ Keep shoulders relaxed
instructions: Stand tall, extend arms to sides.
Make large circles forward for 30 seconds, then reverse for 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Hip Circles
form_tips: ✅ Move from the hips, not the knees
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hands on hips.
Make large circles with your hips — 30 sec clockwise, 30 sec counterclockwise.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squats
form_tips: ✅ Full range of motion — go below parallel
🚫 Don't let knees cave inward
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out.
Lower down as if sitting into a chair — go as deep as comfortable.
Push through heels to stand back up.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow Stretch
form_tips: ✅ Breathe in on the 'cow' (arch), breathe out on the 'cat' (round)
instructions: Get on all fours — hands under shoulders, knees under hips.
Arch your back downward and look up (cow). Then round your back upward and tuck your chin (cat).
Flow between the two positions, controlled breathing.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
form_tips: ✅ Keep arms straight
✅ Squeeze shoulder blades at the back
instructions: Hold a resistance band in front at shoulder height, arms straight.
Pull the band apart by moving hands out to each side until arms are fully extended.
Slowly return to start.

exercises.name: Light Dumbbell Flyes
form_tips: ✅ This is activation only — use 20–30% of your working weight
✅ Feel the stretch at the bottom
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with very light dumbbells.
With a slight bend in elbows, lower arms out wide until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Bring them back together at the top.

exercises.name: Wall Slides
form_tips: ✅ Keep lower back flat against the wall
🚫 Don't let elbows leave the wall
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall, arms at 90° (goalpost position).
Slowly slide your arms up the wall until straight overhead.
Lower back down, keeping contact with the wall throughout.

exercises.name: Dead Hangs
form_tips: ✅ Relax your shoulders and let your lats stretch
✅ Breathe deeply
instructions: Grab a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, shoulder-width.
Let your body hang fully — shoulders decompressing.
Hold for 20 seconds, feet off the ground.

exercises.name: Band-Assisted Lat Stretch
form_tips: ✅ Breathe into the stretch — don't hold your breath
instructions: Anchor a resistance band to a pole or rack at shoulder height.
Grab the band with one hand, step back to create tension.
Shift your hips away to feel a deep lat stretch.
Hold 30 seconds each side.

exercises.name: Leg Swings
form_tips: ✅ Hold a wall for balance
✅ Let the leg swing freely — don't force it
instructions: Stand sideways to a wall, hold for balance.
Swing one leg forward and back 10 times, then side to side 10 times.
Switch legs and repeat.

exercises.name: Glute Bridges
form_tips: ✅ Squeeze glutes hard at the top
🚫 Don't hyperextend your lower back
instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat on floor.
Drive hips up by squeezing glutes — form a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Hold briefly at top, then lower with control.

exercises.name: Walking Lunges
form_tips: ✅ Keep torso upright
🚫 Don't let front knee go past toes
instructions: Step forward into a lunge, lower back knee toward the floor.
Push off front foot to bring back leg forward into the next lunge.
Continue walking for 10 steps each leg.

exercises.name: Static Chest Stretch
form_tips: ✅ Stand in a doorway, arm at 90° and lean forward gently
instructions: Stand in a doorway with your arm bent at 90° against the frame.
Lean your body forward until you feel a stretch across your chest.
Hold for 30 seconds each side.

exercises.name: Lat Stretch (Overhead Lean)
form_tips: ✅ Let gravity do the work — don't force it
instructions: Stand next to a pole or rack.
Grab it at shoulder height, step back and shift your hips away.
Feel the stretch run down your lat.
Hold 30 seconds each side.

exercises.name: Quad Stretch
form_tips: ✅ Hold a wall for balance
✅ Keep knees together
instructions: Stand on one leg, bend the other knee and grab your ankle behind you.
Pull heel toward glutes, keep knees together.
Hold 30 seconds each leg.

exercises.name: Hamstring Stretch
form_tips: ✅ Hinge from hips — don't round your lower back
instructions: Sit on the floor, one leg extended, other foot to inner thigh.
Hinge forward from hips toward extended foot.
Feel the stretch in the back of your leg. Hold 30 seconds, switch sides.

exercises.name: Child's Pose
form_tips: ✅ Let your chest sink toward the floor
✅ Breathe deeply into your lower back
instructions: Kneel on the floor, sit back onto your heels.
Reach arms forward on the floor, let your chest sink down.
Hold for 1 full minute, breathing deeply.

exercises.name: Neck Rolls
form_tips: ✅ Move slowly — no fast jerky movements
instructions: Sit or stand tall, let your chin drop to your chest.
Slowly roll your head to one side, then back, then the other side.
Take 30 seconds to complete a few full slow rolls.

exercises.name: Bench Press
form_tips: 🚫 Don't bounce the bar off your chest
✅ Keep feet flat on the floor, no leg drive
⚠️ If elbows flare past 90°, reduce weight
✅ Keep shoulder blades pinched and retracted throughout
✅ Lower the bar to mid-chest, not neck or stomach
instructions: Set the bench to flat. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width.
Unrack and lower bar to mid-chest with control — take 2 seconds on the way down.
Press explosively back up. Don't lock elbows at the top.
Keep shoulder blades pinched together throughout the movement.
Re-rack carefully — don't drop the bar at the end of a set.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
form_tips: ✅ Set bench to 30–45° — not too steep or it becomes shoulder press
🚫 Don't touch dumbbells at the top — keep tension on chest
⚠️ Control the descent — don't let gravity take over
✅ Lead with your elbows, not your hands
instructions: Set bench to a 30–45 degree incline. Sit with dumbbells on thighs.
Kick dumbbells up to shoulder level as you lie back.
Press dumbbells upward and slightly inward — stop just short of touching at top.
Lower with control, elbows flaring to about 75°.
Bring dumbbells back to thighs when done — don't drop them.

exercises.name: Cable Flyes
form_tips: ✅ Use a constant arc motion — like hugging a big tree
🚫 Don't let the cables pull your arms too far back — control the stretch
⚠️ Keep a slight bend in elbows — never fully lock
✅ Squeeze at the center of chest at peak contraction
instructions: Set cables to chest height (or high for lower chest, low for upper chest).
Step forward, one foot ahead for balance. Arms slightly bent.
Bring handles together in a wide arc, as if hugging a large barrel.
Squeeze chest at the peak, then slowly return with control.
Don't let the stack slam — control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
form_tips: ✅ Keep elbows pointing forward — don't flare them out
🚫 Don't use momentum — controlled movement only
✅ Get a full stretch at the bottom for long head activation
instructions: Hold a dumbbell or EZ-bar with both hands above your head.
Keep upper arms stationary — only your forearms should move.
Lower the weight behind your head until you feel a deep tricep stretch.
Press back up to full extension, squeezing triceps at top.
Keep core braced — don't arch your lower back.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
form_tips: ✅ Keep elbows pinned to your sides — they're the pivot point
🚫 Don't lean forward to use body weight
✅ Fully extend at the bottom — squeeze triceps hard
instructions: Set a cable with a rope or bar attachment at high position.
Grip the attachment, tuck elbows to your sides.
Push downward until arms are fully extended, squeezing triceps.
Control the return — don't let the weight yank your arms up.
Keep upper body still — only your forearms should move.

exercises.name: Lateral Raises
form_tips: ⚠️ Use lighter weight than you think — side delts are small
✅ Lead with your elbows, not your hands
🚫 Don't shrug your traps as you raise — isolate the side delt
✅ Slight forward lean can help isolate the mid-delt
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at sides, slight bend in knees.
Raise arms to the side until they're parallel to the floor — no higher.
Lead with elbows. Pinky finger slightly higher than thumb at top.
Lower slowly — take 2–3 seconds on the way down.
Avoid swinging; keep the movement strict.

exercises.name: Overhead Press
form_tips: ✅ Brace your core hard — this protects your lower back
🚫 Don't press behind your neck — press straight up from collarbone
⚠️ Don't excessively arch lower back to get the bar up
✅ Bar path should be slightly diagonal — tuck chin as bar passes your face
instructions: Set the barbell at collarbone height in a rack. Grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Unrack and hold bar at collarbone/upper chest. Brace your core.
Press straight overhead — tuck your chin slightly so bar clears your face.
Lock out at top, then lower bar back to collarbone with control.
Keep glutes and core tight throughout — don't use leg drive.

exercises.name: Incline Bench Press
form_tips: ✅ Set bench at 30–45° for maximum upper chest emphasis
🚫 Don't let the bar drift toward your neck
✅ Keep wrists stacked directly above your elbows
instructions: Set bench to 30–45°. Grip barbell slightly wider than shoulder width.
Unrack and lower to your upper chest — control the descent.
Press the bar up and slightly back toward the rack.
Keep your shoulder blades retracted and lower back with a natural arch.
Don't bounce the bar off your chest.

exercises.name: Front Raises
form_tips: ✅ Use a neutral or pronated grip
🚫 Don't swing your body — keep the movement strict
⚠️ Don't raise past shoulder height
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs, palms facing down.
Keeping arms almost straight, raise one or both arms to shoulder height.
Pause briefly at top, then lower with control.
Avoid shrugging or using momentum.

exercises.name: Lateral Raises
form_tips: ⚠️ Use lighter weight than you think — side delts are small
✅ Lead with your elbows, not your hands
🚫 Don't shrug your traps as you raise
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at sides, slight bend in knees.
Raise arms to the side until parallel to floor.
Lead with elbows. Pinky slightly higher than thumb at top.
Lower slowly — take 2–3 seconds on the way down.

exercises.name: Tricep Dips
form_tips: ✅ Keep torso upright to emphasize triceps (lean forward = more chest)
🚫 Don't flare elbows excessively — keep them tracking back
⚠️ Stop if you feel sharp shoulder pain
instructions: Grip parallel bars, arms fully extended, body upright.
Lower yourself by bending elbows — keep them tracking backward, not flaring.
Dip until upper arms are parallel to floor or slightly below.
Press back up to full extension.
Add weight via belt if bodyweight becomes too easy.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown
form_tips: ✅ Keep elbows pinned to your sides
✅ Fully extend at the bottom — pause and squeeze
instructions: Attach a rope or bar to the high cable pulley.
Grip the attachment, elbows at sides.
Push downward until arms fully extend.
Squeeze triceps at full extension, then slowly release.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
form_tips: ✅ Pull bar to your collarbone — not behind your neck
🚫 Don't lean back excessively — slight lean is fine
✅ Initiate the pull by depressing your shoulder blades first
⚠️ Control the return — don't let the weight pull you up
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown machine, thighs secured under pads. Grip bar wider than shoulder width.
Lean back very slightly and depress your shoulder blades.
Pull the bar down to your collarbone, driving elbows down and back.
Squeeze lats at the bottom. Hold for 1 second.
Return the bar slowly — take 3 seconds on the way up.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
form_tips: ✅ Drive elbows back — think of squeezing a pencil between your shoulder blades
🚫 Don't use lower back momentum — row with your back
✅ Sit tall — don't round your lower back
⚠️ Don't jerk the weight — controlled, deliberate rows
instructions: Sit at the cable row machine, feet on platform, knees slightly bent.
Grip the handle, sit tall with slight lean forward.
Row the handle to your lower abdomen, driving elbows back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades at the peak. Hold 1 second.
Return to start with control — don't let your torso rock forward.

exercises.name: Single Arm DB Row
form_tips: ✅ Row the dumbbell toward your hip, not your shoulder
✅ Keep your torso parallel to the floor
🚫 Don't rotate your hips to get extra range — keep them square
✅ Full stretch at the bottom — let the lat elongate
instructions: Place one knee and same-side hand on a bench. Opposite foot on floor.
Hold dumbbell with free hand, arm hanging straight down.
Row the dumbbell upward, driving elbow toward your hip.
Squeeze lat at the top. Slowly lower to full extension.
Complete all reps on one side before switching.

exercises.name: Face Pulls
form_tips: ✅ Set cable at face height or slightly higher
✅ Pull toward your face — hands finish beside your ears
🚫 Don't pull to your neck — it changes the muscle target
⚠️ Use light weight — this is a health exercise, not a show exercise
instructions: Set the cable with a rope attachment at face height.
Grip both ends of the rope, step back to create tension.
Pull the rope toward your face, splitting it apart as it reaches you.
Finish with hands beside your ears, elbows high and wide.
Slowly return — don't let the cable snap back.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
form_tips: ✅ Keep elbows pinned at your sides — they're the pivot point
🚫 Don't swing your torso — it's cheating and risks back injury
✅ Squeeze hard at the top of the curl
⚠️ Lower slowly — the eccentric builds more mass
instructions: Stand holding a barbell with an underhand grip, shoulder-width.
Keep elbows pinned to your sides throughout.
Curl the bar upward toward your chest, squeezing biceps at top.
Lower slowly — take 3 seconds on the way down.
Don't lock elbows at the bottom — keep tension on the muscle.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
form_tips: ✅ Neutral grip (thumbs up) throughout the movement
🚫 Don't rotate your wrist — this distinguishes it from a regular curl
✅ Alternate arms or do both simultaneously
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at sides, palms facing your body (neutral/hammer grip).
Curl one or both dumbbells upward — keeping the hammer grip all the way.
Squeeze at the top, elbow stays at your side.
Lower with control. Repeat on other side if alternating.

exercises.name: Deadlift
form_tips: ✅ Keep the bar close to your body — it should drag up your shins
🚫 Don't round your lower back — neutral spine is mandatory
✅ Take a big breath into your belly and brace before each rep
⚠️ If lower back rounds at any weight, reduce the load immediately
✅ Drive through your legs, not just your back
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width, bar over mid-foot. Hinge at hips, grip bar just outside knees.
Take a big breath, brace your core, and 'chest up' to set your back flat.
Drive through the floor with your legs — the bar and your hips should rise together.
Lock out at top: hips through, shoulders back. Don't hyperextend.
Lower the bar with control — hips back first, then bend knees once bar passes them.

exercises.name: Pull-ups
form_tips: ✅ Dead hang at the bottom — full range of motion
✅ Pull your elbows toward your pockets — not just up
🚫 Don't kip or swing — strict reps only
⚠️ Use a band for assistance if you can't do full reps yet
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands just wider than shoulders.
Depress your shoulder blades before initiating the pull.
Pull yourself up until chin clears the bar — think elbows toward your pockets.
Lower yourself fully to a dead hang each rep.
Scale with resistance band assistance if needed.

exercises.name: Cable Row
form_tips: ✅ Squeeze shoulder blades hard at the peak
🚫 Don't lean back to move more weight
✅ Try a wide grip for more upper back / rear delt work
instructions: Sit at the cable row, grip with a wide attachment bar or separate handles.
Row with elbows flaring out at 90° to target upper back.
Squeeze at peak. Control the return.
Keep torso stable — don't rock.

exercises.name: Reverse Flyes
form_tips: ✅ Keep a slight bend in elbows — don't fully lock them
🚫 Don't shrug your traps — this is rear delt isolation
⚠️ Use very light weight — rear delts are small and often weak
instructions: Set cable pulleys to low or use dumbbells, lean forward 45–90°.
With slight elbow bend, raise arms out to the sides.
Squeeze rear delts at the top — think 'spreading your wings'.
Lower with control.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
form_tips: ✅ Don't let elbows leave the pad — this keeps the focus on biceps
🚫 Don't go to full lockout — stop just short to keep tension
⚠️ This is a strict isolation — use lighter weight than barbell curls
instructions: Sit at the preacher curl bench, upper arms flat against the pad.
Grip EZ-bar or dumbbells, start with arms nearly extended.
Curl upward, squeezing biceps hard at the top.
Lower slowly — don't let arms drop — stop just short of full extension.
Upper arms must stay on the pad throughout.

exercises.name: Concentration Curl
form_tips: ✅ Twist wrist slightly outward (supinate) at the top for peak contraction
🚫 Don't swing your arm — brace it against your inner thigh
✅ Squeeze at the top and lower very slowly
instructions: Sit on a bench, legs apart. Hold a dumbbell in one hand, elbow braced on inner thigh.
Let your arm hang nearly straight.
Curl the dumbbell up toward your shoulder — supinate slightly at the top.
Squeeze hard at peak. Lower slowly — 3 seconds down.
Complete all reps then switch arms.

exercises.name: Squat
form_tips: ✅ Brace your core like you're about to take a punch
🚫 Don't let knees cave inward — push them out in line with toes
✅ Break parallel — go below if mobility allows
⚠️ Heels should stay flat on the floor throughout
✅ Take a breath at the top, hold it through the descent and drive
instructions: Set the barbell on your upper traps (high bar) or rear delts (low bar). Step back, feet shoulder-width.
Take a deep breath and brace your core hard.
Break at hips and knees simultaneously — sit back and down.
Hit depth (below parallel if possible), then drive through your heels.
Exhale at the top. Re-rack safely.

exercises.name: Leg Press
form_tips: ⚠️ Never lock out your knees at full extension — keep a slight bend
🚫 Don't let your lower back peel off the seat
✅ High foot placement = more glutes/hamstrings. Low placement = more quads
✅ Full depth — thighs to chest if flexibility allows
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine, feet on platform at shoulder width.
Lower the safety handles. Lower the platform until knees are 90° or below.
Drive through heels to press the platform back up — stop just short of lockout.
Control the descent — don't let the weight drop.
Re-engage the safety handles when done.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
form_tips: ✅ Hinge at the hips — this is not a squat
✅ Keep the bar (or dumbbells) close to your body throughout
🚫 Don't round your lower back — neutral spine is critical
⚠️ Feel the stretch in hamstrings before reversing the movement
instructions: Stand with barbell or dumbbells at hip height, overhand grip.
Push your hips back while keeping knees almost straight (soft bend).
Lower the weight close to your legs, spine neutral, until you feel hamstring stretch.
Drive hips forward to return to standing. Squeeze glutes at the top.
Don't lower past the point your back stays neutral.

exercises.name: Leg Curl
form_tips: ✅ Squeeze hamstrings hard at full curl
🚫 Don't let your hips rise off the pad — keep them flat
⚠️ Control the return — the eccentric is where gains are made
instructions: Lie face down on leg curl machine. Pad positioned just above ankles.
Curl legs upward — squeeze hamstrings hard at the peak.
Hold briefly at full flex.
Lower slowly with control — take 3 seconds on the way down.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
form_tips: ✅ Extend fully and hold for 1 second at top to hit the VMO
⚠️ Don't use this if you have knee pain — consult a physio
🚫 Don't use momentum — slow controlled reps only
instructions: Sit in the leg extension machine, ankles behind the pad.
Grip the handles and extend your legs to full lockout.
Hold at the top for 1 second — feel the quad squeeze.
Lower with complete control — don't drop the weight.

exercises.name: Calf Raises
form_tips: ✅ Get a full stretch at the bottom — go all the way down
✅ Hold at the peak — calves respond well to time under tension
⚠️ Calves are stubborn — higher reps and slow tempo work better
🚫 Don't bounce out of the bottom — full stretch, then rise
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or calf raise machine. Place balls of feet on the platform.
Lower heels fully below the platform level — full stretch.
Rise up onto tiptoes as high as possible.
Hold at the top for 1–2 seconds, then lower with complete control.
Do not bounce out of the bottom position.
